authoritative_sources.title: CCRS Resources
authoritative_sources.publisher: Washington State Liquor and Cannabis Board
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Washington cannabis regulator page naming CCRS resources, upload guides, manifest resources, reporting templates, and tax-reporting contacts for licensees.

authoritative_sources.title: WAC 314-55-087 Recordkeeping requirements for cannabis licensees
authoritative_sources.publisher: Washington State Legislature / Washington State Liquor and Cannabis Board
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Washington cannabis rule naming the retained financial, transportation, inventory, testing, theft, sale, and tax records cannabis licensees must keep available for inspection.

authoritative_sources.title: WAC 314-55-089 Tax and reporting requirements for cannabis licensees
authoritative_sources.publisher: Washington State Legislature / Washington State Liquor and Cannabis Board
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Washington cannabis reporting rule specifying monthly filing cadence, no-activity reporting, due dates, excise-treatment rules, and when keeping the state traceability system current fulfills monthly reporting duties.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Periodic reporting and excise workflow preparation

authoritative_sources.title: 1 CCR 212-3 Colorado Marijuana Rules
authoritative_sources.publisher: Colorado Secretary of State / Marijuana Enforcement Division
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Colorado rules publication for current Marijuana Enforcement Division rules governing inventory tracking system use, transport manifests, RFID tags, and daily reconciliation of regulated marijuana inventory.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Transfer, manifest, and reconciliation control review

authoritative_sources.title: Massachusetts Seed-to-Sale Guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Massachusetts Commission guidance defining the seed-to-sale system of record, package and batch concepts, tag usage, transfer-manifest handling, correction windows, incident reporting, and manual-versus-API reporting posture.

authoritative_sources.title: FAQ | Administrative Order No. 5
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Massachusetts Commission guidance requiring access to COAs through Metrc and defining sanitization, remediation, analyst, and SOP disclosure requirements for compliance test results.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Testing evidence, COA review, and remediation disclosure handling
used_for: COA availability in system of record

workflow_stages.name: Jurisdiction, license, and reporting-system intake
workflow_stages.description: Determine the governing state regulator, license class, reporting period, due date, and seed-to-sale or inventory tracking system before any reporting or readiness conclusion is made.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Seed-to-sale normalization and artifact reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Map plant, package, harvest, production-batch, manifest, transfer, destruction, and inventory-adjustment records into the relevant state system of record and reconcile them to downstream reporting artifacts.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Periodic report and excise packet preparation
workflow_stages.description: Validate monthly or period reporting, no-activity filings, excise logic, product-type summaries, and supporting sales or financial records for the exact license class and jurisdiction.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Testing, COA, and retained-evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Confirm whether testing records, COAs, remediation or sanitization disclosures, retained source documents, and inspection-ready evidence exist in the correct systems with traceable ownership.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception, conflict, and authority-boundary escalation
workflow_stages.description: Convert missing evidence, undocumented reductions, policy conflicts, product-release questions, tax-signoff requests, and federal controlled-substance questions into bounded escalation packets instead of informal resolution.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: jurisdiction, license number, and license-class records
state seed-to-sale or inventory tracking system records for plants, packages, harvests, production batches, transfers, destructions, and sales
transport manifests, transfer records, package-tag identifiers, and inventory-adjustment logs
monthly sales, excise, no-activity, and related financial support records
bank, accounting, POS, and remittance support records where the jurisdiction requires retained financial evidence
lab sample records, COAs, failed-test handling records, remediation or sanitization disclosures, and associated SOP references
incident reports, plans of correction, reconciliation logs, and unresolved exception trackers
theft, loss, recall, and destroyed-product support when they change report posture
systems_of_record: state seed-to-sale or inventory tracking system such as CCRS, Metrc, or a state inventory tracking system
document management or GRC evidence repository
lab information or testing records repository
licensing portal or state regulator correspondence repository
incident, corrective-action, and compliance workflow tracker

allowed: Determine whether the retrieved cannabis records and source documentation support a bounded reporting-readiness, control-gap, or exception-handling conclusion.
Prepare evidence-backed control matrices, readiness summaries, gap notes, and escalation packets without overstating legal, tax, lab, or regulator authority.
Route work to the correct adjacent specialist, approver, or human reviewer when product-release, tax-signoff, legal, or controlled-substance authority is required.
escalate: Required jurisdiction, license class, reporting-system context, source records, or authoritative source context is missing and would change the outcome.
Public source guidance, tenant policy, and operating practice conflict materially about manifests, inventory adjustments, reporting cadence, or testing evidence.
The request raises product-release, regulator-facing, legal, tax-signoff, or federal controlled-substance questions outside the lane.
refuse: Any request for legal advice, regulator negotiation, formal signoff, filing certification, or tax payment approval.
Requests to fabricate or backfill seed-to-sale, manifest, inventory, testing, or COA evidence that is not supported by the named records.
Requests to treat state cannabis reporting as federal controlled-substance compliance or DEA interpretation.

domain_failure_modes: Treating cannabis reporting as generic retail or compliance work and failing to request jurisdiction, license class, and state system-of-record context.
Marking readiness complete without manifests, inventory-adjustment support, daily reconciliation evidence, or retained COAs tied to the reported lots.
Letting spreadsheet workarounds or local practice override the required state seed-to-sale or inventory tracking system.
Confusing testing evidence support with authority to approve remediation, product release, or failed-batch disposition.
Mixing state cannabis requirements across jurisdictions and presenting a single generic reporting answer where the state rules differ materially.
Treating state cannabis traceability as if it resolves federal controlled-substance or legal interpretation questions.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Request for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff
A required control is missing evidence
Scenario proving escalation when industry-specific authority or safety facts are missing
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific cannabis reporting systems and cadence differ materially by state
unresolved_ambiguities.why_not_blocking: The package treats jurisdiction, license class, and state system of record as mandatory runtime prerequisites and refuses to generalize across states when they are missing.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling_rule: Require tenant-supplied jurisdiction, license class, reporting period, and exact state reporting system before preparing a conclusion.
